description:
A junior penetration tester left you a Windows-encoded report that is hard to read on Linux.  
    Your job is to convert that report to UTF-8 and record a tiny audit trail.

    Current situation (already present on disk):
      • Directory     : /home/user/scan_outputs  
      • File          : /home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf16le.txt  
        – Encoding    : UTF-16 Little Endian WITH a BOM  
        – Text lines  : three lines of plain ASCII letters/numbers separated by LF
                       (because the bytes are UTF-16LE each LF is two bytes: 0x0A00)  
        – Visible content after conversion to UTF-8 would look exactly like:  
            Open port: 22/ssh  
            Deprecated protocol: SSLv3 detected  
            Vulnerability: CVE-2023-1234  

    Tasks you must accomplish (use no more than 3–4 shell commands in total):

      1. Create a UTF-8 version of the file called /home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf8.txt.  
         • The textual content must be *identical* (three lines, trailing LF on each).  
         • The file must NOT contain a BOM.  
         • File permissions must remain user-readable (0600 or 0644 are both acceptable).

      2. Produce the audit trail file /home/user/scan_outputs/conversion.log with EXACTLY
         the three lines shown below and nothing else.  The automated checker is
         case-sensitive and will fail on additional whitespace, blank lines, or extra
         characters.

            source_file=/home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf16le.txt
            converted_file=/home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf8.txt
            lines_in_converted_file=3

         • No additional spaces before or after the equals sign.  
         • A trailing newline *is* required at the end of the third line.

    Verification the grader will perform:

      • cat /home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf8.txt  – must show the three UTF-8 lines
        exactly as listed above, each terminated by one LF, no extra bytes, no BOM.  

      • wc -l /home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf8.txt – must print “3 …”  

      • diff -q /home/user/scan_outputs/conversion.log <(printf 'source_file=/home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf16le.txt\nconverted_file=/home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf8.txt\nlines_in_converted_file=3\n')  
        – must report no differences.

    Reminder: do not delete or rename the original UTF-16LE file.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file created by the harness -->
    File: /home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf16le.txt  
    Encoding: UTF-16LE with BOM (0xFF 0xFE prefix)  
    Content in UTF-8 (for reference only):  
        "Open port: 22/ssh\nDeprecated protocol: SSLv3 detected\nVulnerability: CVE-2023-1234\n"

    <!-- Expected artefacts after the agent finishes -->
    1. /home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf8.txt  
       – Encoding: UTF-8, NO BOM  
       – Exact byte sequence:  
           4f 70 65 6e 20 70 6f 72 74 3a 20 32 32 2f 73 73 68 0a
           44 65 70 72 65 63 61 74 65 64 20 70 72 6f 74 6f 63 6f 6c 3a 20 53 53 4c 76 33 20 64 65 74 65 63 74 65 64 0a
           56 75 6c 6e 65 72 61 62 69 6c 69 74 79 3a 20 43 56 45 2d 32 30 32 33 2d 31 32 33 34 0a

    2. /home/user/scan_outputs/conversion.log  
       – Exact text (with a final LF after the third line):  
           source_file=/home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf16le.txt\n
           converted_file=/home/user/scan_outputs/vuln_scan_utf8.txt\n
           lines_in_converted_file=3\n
name: task_000000_c2be1abb
